title: install.sh prebuilt-download-with-fallback, dev-deploy.sh order fix, stale org fix
action: Rework install.sh (per D 3168932d). Current install.sh:22-23 hard-exits if cargo/npm are missing BEFORE anything else runs (`command -v cargo >/dev/null || { echo ...; exit 1; }`, same for npm) — this prerequisite gate MUST move so it only applies inside the source-build fallback branch, otherwise a toolchain-less machine (exactly who the download path is for) never reaches the download attempt at all, defeating D 3168932d's whole point. Sequence: (1) detect the host target string matching release.yml's matrix (x86_64-unknown-linux-musl, aarch64-unknown-linux-musl, aarch64-apple-darwin — via uname -s/-m, same mapping style as mdview's install.sh detect_target(), no checksum verification per D 3168932d); (2) attempt a curl download of the matching release tarball asset (URL pattern: https://github.com/vantt/herdr-gateway/releases/latest/download/herdr-gateway-<target-string>.tar.gz) into a temp dir (respect an optional HERDCTL_VERSION env var to pin a version instead of latest, mirroring mdview's MDVIEW_VERSION) — no cargo/npm check needed for this step; (3) if the download+extract succeeds and yields an executable herdctl binary, use that binary directly (skip the compile toolchain entirely) — install it, but keep sourcing packaging/herdr-gateway.service and config.example.json from REPO_DIR (the local checkout) as today, since those are stable templates not build artifacts; (4) only if download fails or the platform is unrecognized, THEN run the cargo/npm prerequisite check (relocated from the old lines 22-23) and fall through to the existing from-source path, with its step order swapped: bundle the web UI first (npm install && npm run bundle inside web/), THEN compile the release binary — required now because embedding happens at compile time (embed-pkg-2). Also delete the now-unnecessary 'Installing web assets' section that copies static into the install prefix, and the config.example.json static_dir sed-rewrite (embedded assets are the default; static_dir stays at its harmless default). Apply the identical bundle-before-compile reorder to dev-deploy.sh (it always builds from source, no download path needed there — its cargo/npm prereq check at the top stays where it is, unchanged). In packaging/herdr-gateway.service, fix the stale Documentation= URL pointing at the wrong GitHub org (thanhsmind) to the actual origin remote's org (vantt) — a one-line incidental correctness fix found while touching this file, unrelated to the embedding/install work itself; call this out explicitly as a separate line item when reporting the diff, not folded silently into the install.sh changes. Note for the worker: the verify command actually runs ./install.sh for real on this machine (no GitHub release exists yet, so this exercises the fallback-to-source-build branch, which is exactly why the relocated prereq check must still work) — this requires a working Rust + Node toolchain and systemd --user available in the environment, same prerequisites this repo's existing install.sh has always had.

truths: Running install.sh today (no GitHub release exists yet for this repo) exercises the real download-fails-then-falls-back-to-source path end to end and still succeeds
install.sh no longer copies a static assets directory anywhere or rewrites static_dir in the generated config
dev-deploy.sh bundles the web UI before compiling the release binary
The cargo/npm prerequisite check no longer runs before the download attempt — it only fires inside the source-build fallback branch

The download URL is built with the HERDCTL_VERSION/target values quoted, curl runs with -fSL and --proto '=https' (never falls through to compile-time errors on a 404 HTML body treated as a binary), and extraction happens into an isolated temp dir

artifacts.substantive: target detection, download attempt with HERDCTL_VERSION override (quoted, https-only, isolated temp-dir extraction) runs before any toolchain check, graceful fallback to (relocated prereq check +) bundle-then-compile, no static copy step
bundle step precedes the compile step

key_links: install.sh's fallback path still bundles the web UI before compiling the release binary
prohibitions: Config/secrets/systemd-unit-writing sections of install.sh stay functionally unchanged
The cargo/npm prerequisite check is not deleted, only relocated to gate the fallback branch

trace.outcome: Reworked install.sh to try a prebuilt-release download first (target detection, quoted+https-only curl into an isolated temp dir, HERDCTL_VERSION override) with the cargo/npm prereq check relocated to gate only the source-build fallback; removed the static-assets copy and static_dir config rewrite; reordered dev-deploy.sh to bundle before compile; fixed a stale org URL in the systemd unit. Verified with a real ./install.sh run on this machine (no release exists yet, so it genuinely exercised detect->download-fails->fallback-to-source-build->install, exit 0).

deviations.description: Documentation= URL pointed at the stale org 'thanhsmind' instead of the repo's actual origin org 'vantt' (confirmed via git remote -v). Fixed as a one-line incidental correctness fix, called out separately from the install.sh rework per the cell's instruction.

Removed the now-unused SHARE_DIR variable and its mkdir — it was only ever used for the deleted static-assets copy and the deleted static_dir config rewrite. data_dir() (embed-pkg-1) resolves independently via XDG_DATA_HOME and creates its own directory at runtime, so install.sh no longer needs to pre-create it.

trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": ["install.sh (no automated test framework — shell script, verified by real execution)", "dev-deploy.sh (syntax + read-through)"],
  "tests_added_or_changed": [],
  "red_failure_evidence": "Prior install.sh unconditionally required cargo+npm before anything else (lines 22-23), always built from source, copied static/ into $SHARE_DIR/static, and sed-rewrote static_dir in the generated config to point at that copy — defeating the point of a prebuilt-binary download path (a toolchain-less machine would exit before ever reaching a download attempt) and no longer matching D b300856d's embedded-UI-by-default model. No pre-existing automated test covered this file; behavior was characterized by reading the prior script (git show HEAD:install.sh) and the plan/approach docs' discovery notes, not a failing test.",
  "verification_run": "bash -n install.sh && bash -n dev-deploy.sh && ./install.sh -> exit 0. Real run on this machine (no GitHub release/tag exists) produced: 'Detected target: x86_64-unknown-linux-musl' -> 'no matching prebuilt release found; building from source' -> bundled web UI (vite build succeeded) -> compiled herdctl (release) -> installed binary to ~/.local/bin/herdctl -> kept existing config/secrets (idempotent, unchanged) -> installed systemd unit -> enabled service. This is the real fallback branch: download genuinely 404s (no release exists), and the cargo/npm prereq check only fired after the download attempt failed, confirming the relocation. Post-run sanity checks: ~/.local/bin/herdctl runs and prints its usage banner; ~/.local/share/herdr-gateway (the old static-assets copy target) was NOT created, confirming the static copy step is fully gone.",
  "deliberate_exceptions": ["The download-succeeds branch (detect_target + download_prebuilt actually finding and extracting a real release tarball) is unverified end-to-end this session, per the plan's own risk map — no GitHub release/tag exists yet for this repo. Verified by code inspection instead: curl -fSL --proto '=https', tar extraction into an isolated mktemp -d, and a check that the expected herdctl path exists inside the extracted tarball before using it."]
}
